volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Two Bacchises (Section 2.2.220b - Section 2.3.303b)

sections.label: Section 2.2.220b
sections.translation: Yes , some Philippeans .

sections.label: Section 2.2.221
sections.translation: And you have need of it directly , perhaps ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.221b
sections.translation: Aye , and even sooner than directly . For there ’ s a Captain coming here just now —

sections.label: Section 2.2.222b
sections.translation: A Captain , indeed !

sections.label: Section 2.2.223
sections.original: Qui de amittenda Bacchide aurum hic exiget .
sections.translation: Who is demanding some gold here for relinquishing Bacchis .

sections.label: Section 2.2.224
sections.original: Veniat quando volt , atque ita ne mihi sit morae .
sections.translation: Let him come when he pleases , and so there be no delay .

sections.label: Section 2.2.225
sections.original: domist : non metuo nec ego quoiquam supplico ,
sections.translation: The money ’ s at home ; I fear not for myself , nor do I go begging to any man ; so long , at least , as this heart of mine shall be armed with its inventiveness . Go in ; I ’ ll manage here . Do you tell them in-doors , that Mnesilochus is coming to Bacchis .

sections.label: Section 2.2.228b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do as you request .

sections.label: Section 2.2.229
sections.original: Negotium hoc ad me adtinet aurarium .
sections.translation: The money business belongs to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.230
sections.original: mille et ducentos Philippos áttulimus aureos
sections.translation: From Ephesus we have brought twelve hundred golden Philippeans , which our entertainer owed to our old gentleman . Hence , some contrivance [*] will I this day contrive , to procure gold for this son of my master thus in love . But there ’ s a noise at our door — who ’ s coming out of doors , I wonder ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.235
sections.original: Ibo in Piraeum , visam écquae advenerit
sections.translation: To the Piraeus will I go ; I ’ ll go see whether any merchant-ship has come into harbour from Ephesus . For my mind misgives me ; my son lingers there so long , and does not return .

sections.label: Section 2.3.239
sections.original: Extexam ego illum pulchre iam , si di volunt .
sections.translation: Now , I ’ ll finely unravel him , if the Gods are propitious .

sections.label: Section 2.3.240
sections.original: haud dormitandumst : opus est chryso Chrysalo .
sections.translation: There must be no sleeping ; gold is requisite [*] for Chrysalus . I ’ ll accost him , whom for sure this day I ’ ll make a ram of Phryxus [*] of ; so , even to the quick [*] , will I shear him of his gold . ( Accosting him .) His servant Chrysalus salutes Nicobulus .

sections.label: Section 2.3.244
sections.original: Pro di immortales , Chrysale , ubi mist filius ?
sections.translation: O , immortal Gods ! where is my son ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.245
sections.original: Quin tu salutem primum reddis quam dedi ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you return the salutation first , which I gave you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.246
sections.translation: Well , save you . But where on earth is Mnesilochus ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.246b
sections.translation: He is alive and well .

sections.label: Section 2.3.247
sections.translation: Is he not come ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.247b
sections.translation: He is come .

sections.label: Section 2.3.247c
sections.translation: Hurra ! you ’ ve brought me to [*] my senses . And has he all along been well ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.248b
sections.translation: Aye , well as a boxer [*] and an athlete .

sections.label: Section 2.3.249
sections.original: Quid hoc ? qua causa éum in Ephesum miseram ,
sections.translation: But what as to this — the business on account or which I sent him hence to Ephesus ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.250
sections.translation: Has he rot received the gold from his entertainer , Archidemides ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.251
sections.original: Heu , cor meum et cerebrum , Nicobule , finditur ,
sections.translation: Alas ! my heart and my brain are cleft , Nicobulus , whenever mention is made of that fellow . Why don ’ t you call that entertainer of his your enemy ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.254
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.254b
sections.translation: Because , i ’ faith , I know for sure ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.255
sections.original: Volcanus , Luna , Sol , Dies , dei quattuor ,
sections.translation: that Vulcan , the Moon , the Sun , the Day , those four Divinities , never shone upon another more wicked .

sections.label: Section 2.3.257
sections.translation: What , than Archidemides ? Dear me !

sections.label: Section 2.3.257b
sections.translation: Than Archidemides , I say .

sections.label: Section 2.3.258
sections.translation: What has he done ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.258b
sections.original: Quid non fecit ? quin tu id me rogas ?
sections.translation: What has he not done ? Why don ’ t you ask me that ? In the first place , then , he began to make denials to your son ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.260
sections.translation: and to assert that he didn ’ t owe three obols [*] to you . Forthwith , Mnesilochus summoned to himself there our ancient host , the old gentleman , Pelago ; in his presence , he at once showed the fellow the token [*] , which you yourself had given to your son to deliver to him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.265
sections.translation: Well — when he showed him the token ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.265b
sections.translation: He began to say that it was counterfeit , and that it was not a true token ; and how many reproaches he did utter against him so undeserving of them ! and he said that in other matters he had committed forgery .

sections.label: Section 2.3.269
sections.original: Habetin aurum ? íd mihi dici volo .
sections.translation: Have you not the gold ? In the first place , I want that to be told me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.270
sections.translation: Yes , after the Praetor had appointed delegates [*] ; being cast , at length compelled by force he paid down twelve hundred Philippeans .

sections.label: Section 2.3.272b
sections.translation: He owed that much .

sections.label: Section 2.3.273
sections.original: Porro etiam ausculta pugnam quam voluit dare .
sections.translation: Besides , listen to another struggle of his , as well , which he was desirous to enter on .

sections.label: Section 2.3.274
sections.translation: What , besides , as well ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.274b
sections.translation: Oho ! this will turn out now a regular hawk ’ s nest [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.3.275
sections.original: Deceptus sum , Autolyco hospiti aurum credidi .
sections.translation: I ’ ve been deceived . I ’ ve entrusted my gold to an Autolycus [*] for my host !

sections.label: Section 2.3.276
sections.translation: Nay , but do you listen —

sections.label: Section 2.3.276b
sections.original: Immo ingenium avidi haud pernoram hospitis .
sections.translation: Well , I wasn ’ t aware of the disposition of my avaricious entertainer .

sections.label: Section 2.3.277
sections.original: Postquam aurum abstulimus , in navem conscendimus ,
sections.translation: Afterwards , at last we had got the gold , and embarked on board ship , desirous for home . By chance , as I was sitting on the deck , while I was looking about me , at that moment I beheld

sections.label: Section 2.3.280
sections.translation: a long bark being fitted out by this cheating knave .

sections.label: Section 2.3.281
sections.original: Perii hercle , lembus ille mihi laedit latus .
sections.translation: Troth , I ’ m undone ; that bark breaks my heart [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.3.282
sections.original: Is erat communis cum hospite et praedonibus .
sections.translation: This was held in partnership by your host and some pirates .

sections.label: Section 2.3.283
sections.original: Adeon me fuisse fungum , ut qui illi crederem ,
sections.translation: And that I should be such a blockhead as to trust him , when his very name of Archidemides [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.285
sections.original: clamaret dempturum esse , si quid crederem ?
sections.translation: cried aloud to me that he would deprive me of it , if I should entrust anything to him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.286
sections.original: Is lembus nostrae navi insidias dabat .
sections.translation: This bark was lying in wait for our ship . I began to watch them , to see what business they were about . Meanwhile , from harbour our ship set sail . When we had fully left the harbour , these fellows began to follow with their oars ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.290
sections.original: neque aves neque venti citius . quoniam sentio
sections.translation: nor birds , nor winds more swiftly . As I discovered what scheme was being carried out , at once we dropped anchor . As they beheld us stopping , they began to keep their vessel back in harbour .

sections.label: Section 2.3.293b
sections.translation: Wicked wretches , by my troth . What did you do at last ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.294b
sections.translation: We returned again into harbour .

sections.label: Section 2.3.295
sections.original: Sapienter factum a vobis . quid illi postea ?
sections.translation: ’ Twas cleverly done by you . What after that ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.296
sections.translation: At nightfall they returned ashore .

sections.label: Section 2.3.297
sections.original: Aurum hercle auferre voluere : ei rei operam dabant .
sections.translation: Troth now , they intended to carry off the gold ; they were attempting that plan , no doubt .

sections.label: Section 2.3.298
sections.original: Non me fefellit , sensi , eo exanimatus fui .
sections.translation: It didn ’ t escape me ; I discovered it : I was almost terrified to death by it . As I saw that a scheme was being laid against the gold ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.300
sections.translation: forthwith on this we came to this determination ; the next day we carried away all the gold thence in their presence , openly and publicly , that they might know it was done .

sections.label: Section 2.3.303
sections.translation: Skilfully managed , i ’ faith . Tell me , what did they ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.303b
sections.translation: Chopfallen at once ,— soon as ever they saw us leaving the harbour with the gold ,
